report_name: Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket at HealthTech Startup Cura360 Exposes 250,000 Patient Records

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: An external party, a security researcher, accessed sensitive patient data that was left exposed in a publicly accessible AWS S3 bucket belonging to Cura360.

vulnerabilities.name: Public AWS S3 Bucket Misconfiguration
vulnerabilities.description: An AWS S3 bucket at Cura360 containing sensitive patient data was misconfigured for public access, allowing its contents to be viewed and downloaded by anyone. This is a critical cloud security misconfiguration that led to the data exposure.

identities.name: Cura360
identities.description: A health-tech startup that exposed approximately 250,000 patient records due to a misconfigured public AWS S3 bucket.

identities.name: Cura360 Patients
identities.description: Approximately 250,000 patients whose protected health information (PHI) and personal data, including medical records, were exposed in the Cura360 S3 bucket leak.
